body.title: Librarian: 'new agent' creation skill — questions → scaffold from template
body.result: Built skills/new-agent/ — 5-question scaffold CLI copies devices/template/ with class renames, json.dumps injection, yaml profile; hyphens normalised to underscores for valid Python imports
body.description:
When someone wants to add a new agent to the rack, they currently copy devices/template/ manually and edit files by hand. Build a Librarian skill (or CC skill) that asks 4-5 questions (agent name, purpose in one sentence, which rack services it needs, whether it's long-running or stateless) and scaffolds a new device directory from devices/template/ with the answers filled in. Similar to 'claude --init' or 'aider --new-agent'.

Output: a complete devices/<name>/ directory with device.py, shim.py, __init__.py, and a config/profiles/<name>.yaml pre-populated. The new device must pass 'pip install -e .' without errors.

**Affected files:** devices/template/ (read-only seed), skills/ (new scaffold skill), devices/installer/ (may need manifest update)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; template device is the seed — do not modify it, copy and fill
**Scope boundary:** IN — scaffold directory from answered questions; OUT — auto-registration on live rack, automatic test generation
**Completion criteria:** running the scaffold skill with test answers produces a devices/test-agent/ directory that passes 'pip install -e .' and imports without error
